pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 13, export_result.sh line 10
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 14, export_result.sh line 11
Code analysis: verifier.py line 34, scripts confirm 30KB is reasonable minimum for PNG screenshots

Downloaded and inspected https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip: Contains DICOM files in 0051/ directory. DICOM metadata confirms real CT scan labeled 'CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample' (Modality: CT, BodyPartExamined: BASE 3MM, SliceThickness: 2.0mm, StudyDate: 20020604)

privileged_info_summary: Task uses real medical imaging data: CT Cranium (case 0051) from official InVesalius sample datasets. Data comprises 109 DICOM files totaling ~57MB, containing a real CT scan of a cranium at 2mm slice thickness dated 2002-06-04. Output files are standard PNG screenshots from 3D volume rendering exported to /home/ga/Documents/ with a minimum file size threshold of 30KB per PNG. The dataset comes from the official InVesalius GitHub releases and is publicly available real medical data, not synthetic.
